title: Morning Robotics Scan
report:
## China\'s dextrous robotic hands

### The Breakthrough
Chinese startups LinkerBot and Wuji Technology are mass‑producing fully dextrous robotic hands at a rate of about 5,000 units per month and targeting a $1,000 price point for prosthetic versions; Wuji\'s glove captures motion, pressure, and touch data to fuel large‑scale teleoperation training, leveraging China\'s supply chain for miniaturized motors and batteries.

### The Caveats
The article provides only production numbers and price targets, without any independent verification of the hands\' real‑world performance or empirical comparisons to existing solutions.

## NASA\'s LINK spacecraft rescue robot

### The Breakthrough
NASA\'s LINK spacecraft carries a three‑armed robotic manipulator the size of a fridge, equipped with multiple cameras and sensors that autonomously approach and attach to the non‑cooperative Swift telescope, then uses thrusters to raise its orbit, marking the first on‑orbit servicing mission of this kind.

### The Caveats
The three‑arm capture capability has never been demonstrated in space, so its performance and reliability remain completely unproven.

## WorldSample: Closed‑loop Real‑robot RL with World Modelling

### The Breakthrough
WorldSample augments real robot rollouts with a post‑trained world model that generates high‑fidelity synthetic transitions; a Policy‑Paced Learning scheduler selects samples, yielding a 28% increase in task success rate and a 59% reduction in training steps, with visual fidelity improvements of 19.4 dB PSNR and 0.47 SSIM.

### The Caveats
The claimed 28% success increase and 59% training‑step reduction are unconvincing because no concrete baseline methods are named for comparison.

## QuadRocket: Low‑cost quadrotor testbed for adaptive thrust‑vector control

### The Breakthrough
QuadRocket models a rocket‑like vehicle on a quadrotor platform with a thrust‑vector actuator mounted via a universal joint; an adaptive backstepping controller provides almost‑global trajectory tracking and a dynamic‑surface attitude controller tracks the thrust vector, demonstrated in indoor motion‑capture experiments.

### The Caveats
The work provides no comparison to existing thrust‑vector control baselines, leaving the claimed almost‑global tracking performance uncontextualized.

## VT‑WAM: Visual‑Tactile World Action Model for Contact‑Rich Manipulation

### The Breakthrough
VT‑WAM introduces Asymmetric Mixture‑of‑Transformers attention to fuse a first‑frame visual anchor with tactile dynamics, and a contact‑gated Action‑Visual‑Tactile Attention Guidance that focuses actions on tactile evidence, achieving a 71.67% average success rate across six real‑world tasks.

### The Caveats
The reported gains are confined to six specific tasks on a single robot, with no evidence that VT‑WAM generalizes to other platforms or larger‑scale deployments.

## AutowareFoundation Vision Pilot: Open‑source L2 ADAS stack

### The Breakthrough
Vision Pilot combines perception models (AutoSpeed, AutoSteer) with an end‑to‑end model (AutoDrive) in a hybrid AI architecture that runs on a single front‑facing monocular camera, delivering ACC, FCW, AEB, LKAS, LDW, ISA, and lane‑keeping without high‑definition maps; all components are provided as ONNX models with optional ROS 2 support.

### The Caveats
The system is claimed to be production‑ready and safety‑certifiable, yet it has never been tested on real hardware nor compared against existing ADAS stacks.

## Embodied.cpp: Portable C++ Runtime for Embodied AI Models

### The Breakthrough
Embodied.cpp abstracts vision‑language‑action and world‑action model execution into five modular layers and offers fused batch‑1 inference with latency‑first scheduling; experiments show HY‑VLA and pi0.5 models achieve 100% and 91% closed‑loop task success, while a LingBot‑VA transformer reduces memory usage from 312.2 MiB to 88.1 MiB.

### The Caveats
The work provides no comparison to existing inference runtimes, so it\'s unclear whether Embodied.cpp actually delivers the claimed portability or efficiency gains in practice.

## OpenSCvx: JAX‑backed Python library for successive convexification

### The Breakthrough
OpenSCvx combines JAX\'s just‑in‑time compilation, vectorization, and autodiff with a CVXPY‑based convex solver to accelerate the successive convex programming loop, offering a NumPy‑style symbolic API and optional GPU support for rapid trajectory‑optimization prototyping.

### The Caveats
The claimed high‑performance, general‑purpose nature of OpenSCvx is unsubstantiated, as the repository provides no benchmark comparisons to existing solvers or hardware experiments.

## Monty: Open‑source sensorimotor learning framework

### The Breakthrough
Monty is the first public implementation of the Thousand Brains theory\'s sensorimotor architecture, modeling cortical columns as repeating functional units and providing benchmark suites for tasks like object manipulation and navigation.

### The Caveats
The library reports results only in simulation and offers no comparison to established deep‑learning baselines, so its claimed robustness and speed are unsubstantiated.

## Japan\'s Humanoid Robot Legacy Threatened by China

### The Breakthrough
Geminoid HI‑16 integrates a large‑language model trained on creator Hiroshi Ishiguro\'s writings for advanced conversational interaction, while the AI Robot Association reports an 80,000‑hour dataset of remote‑operated mobile manipulation and Vision‑Language‑Action models built on that data.

### The Caveats
The piece claims Chinese humanoids now dominate and that Japan\'s dataset is unrivaled, yet provides no quantitative comparisons, benchmarks, or independent evaluation.

### Priority
The Chinese dextrous‑hand effort leads today because it couples rapid, large‑scale hardware production with rich manipulation data, directly tackling the hardest problem in embodied AI.

### One action
Track the upcoming performance demos from LinkerBot and Wuji; if they release open data or benchmarks, consider integrating their sensor‑rich glove streams into your manipulation pipelines.

### What to watch
WorldSample, VT‑WAM, and QuadRocket all target faster, more reliable learning for contact‑rich manipulation—WorldSample leverages synthetic data from a world model, VT‑WAM fuses visual and tactile streams with novel attention, and QuadRocket provides a low‑cost hardware testbed for thrust‑vector control; watch for cross‑validation studies that compare these approaches on shared tasks.
section_titles: China's startup boom targets dextrous robotic hands
NASA launches LINK spacecraft with three‑armed robot to rescue Swift telescope
WorldSample: Closed-loop Real-robot RL with World Modelling
QuadRocket: low‑cost quadrotor testbed for adaptive thrust‑vector control of rocket‑like vehicles
VT-WAM: Visual‑Tactile World Action Model for Contact‑Rich Manipulation
autowarefoundation Vision Pilot: open-source L2 ADAS stack with hybrid end‑to‑end AI
Embodied.cpp: Portable C++ Runtime for Embodied AI Models on Heterogeneous Robots
OpenSCvx: JAX‑backed Python library for successive convexification trajectory optimization
Monty: Open-source sensorimotor learning framework based on the Thousand Brains theory
Japan’s Humanoid Robot Legacy Threatened by China
